entries.title: Luke Rebuilds Jedi With Limited Knowledge

context.label: Post-Endor New Republic before the New Jedi Order
context.phase: Early New Republic to New Jedi Order Threshold

content.fact: Luke’s post-Endor mission is rebuilding the Jedi with fragments, visions, recovered lore, and hard-earned mistakes.
content.injection: Write Luke as powerful and sincere but institutionally isolated; he is rebuilding a tradition he never fully inherited. Use this as a background rule only; narrower active era/source cards should override it.
constraints: Apply only when the active Saga Context overlaps: Post-Endor New Republic before the New Jedi Order.
Keep retrieval topic/entity-gated; this wide-context card should not flood unrelated scenes.
antiLore: Do not skip ahead to later character, faction, relationship, location, or event states.

context.label: Post-Endor New Republic before the New Jedi Order
context.phase: Early New Republic to New Jedi Order Threshold

content.fact: Leia is Force-sensitive and a Skywalker, but Legends often keeps her primary obligations political, diplomatic, and familial.
content.injection: Do not make Leia’s Jedi training outrank her New Republic and family duties unless the scene context explicitly centers Force training. Use this as a background rule only; narrower active era/source cards should override it.

entries.title: Han Keeps Smuggler Voice Inside State Power

context.label: Post-Endor New Republic before the New Jedi Order
context.phase: Early New Republic to New Jedi Order Threshold

content.fact: Han remains irreverent and improvisational even as he becomes husband, father, general, and New Republic hero.
content.injection: Write Han as changed by commitment without losing his anti-bureaucratic, practical, risk-taking voice. Use this as a background rule only; narrower active era/source cards should override it.
constraints: Apply only when the active Saga Context overlaps: Post-Endor New Republic before the New Jedi Order.
Keep retrieval topic/entity-gated; this wide-context card should not flood unrelated scenes.
antiLore: Do not solve the scene by ignoring this rule unless user-authored continuity overrides it.

entries.title: Mara Moves From Emperor’s Hand to Independent Agent

content.fact: Mara’s arc moves from former Emperor’s Hand and smuggler associate toward independent agency and eventual Jedi path.
content.injection: In New Republic-era Mara scenes, preserve her competence, guardedness, and Imperial past without making her instantly assimilated.
constraints: Apply only when the active Saga Context overlaps: Thrawn Campaign.
Respect story-position bounds from Thrawn Returns to Thrawn Defeated.
antiLore: Do not skip ahead to later character, faction, relationship, location, or event states.

content.injection: Use Karrde as a broker whose word, sources, and neutrality matter more than raw firepower. Do not import later-era status, relationships, factions, or outcomes into this window.

entries.title: Wedge Is Not a Force-Sensitive Solution

context.label: Post-Endor New Republic before the New Jedi Order
context.phase: Early New Republic to New Jedi Order Threshold

content.fact: Wedge remains one of the galaxy’s best non-Force military leaders and pilots.
content.injection: Do not solve Wedge scenes with Jedi powers; his value is command, piloting, trust, and institutional competence. Use this as a background rule only; narrower active era/source cards should override it.
constraints: Apply only when the active Saga Context overlaps: Post-Endor New Republic before the New Jedi Order.
Keep retrieval topic/entity-gated; this wide-context card should not flood unrelated scenes.

entries.title: Corran Combines Investigator, Pilot, and Jedi Roles

context.label: Jedi Academy founding era
context.phase: Jedi Academy Era to Mid New Republic

content.fact: Corran’s identity draws from CorSec investigation, Rogue Squadron combat, family obligations, and later Jedi training.
content.injection: When Corran appears, use his investigative habits and skepticism alongside any Force development. Do not import later-era status, relationships, factions, or outcomes into this window.
constraints: Apply only when the active Saga Context overlaps: Jedi Academy founding era.
Respect story-position bounds from Luke Founds the Jedi Academy to Jedi Praxeum Stabilizes.

entries.title: Kyp’s Guilt After the Sun Crusher Matters

context.label: Exar Kun Defeated at Yavin IV

content.fact: Kyp’s post-crisis identity is marked by guilt, power, and the question of whether redemption can follow catastrophic misuse.
content.injection: After the Sun Crusher crisis, do not write Kyp as consequence-free; his redemption and distrust around him should remain active.
constraints: Apply only when the active Saga Context overlaps: Exar Kun Defeated at Yavin IV.
Respect story-position bounds from Exar Kun Defeated at Yavin IV to Exar Kun Defeated at Yavin IV.

entries.title: Jacen and Jaina Are Children Before NJO

context.label: Post-Endor New Republic before the New Jedi Order
context.phase: Early New Republic to New Jedi Order Threshold

content.fact: Jacen and Jaina are children for much of the New Republic deck, not yet their later NJO-era teenage heroes.
content.injection: Before the NJO threshold, avoid writing Jacen and Jaina with their later war-trauma personalities, romances, or mature Jedi roles. Use this as a background rule only; narrower active era/source cards should override it.
constraints: Apply only when the active Saga Context overlaps: Post-Endor New Republic before the New Jedi Order.

entries.title: Anakin Solo Is a Child Before NJO

content.fact: Anakin Solo exists as the youngest Solo child before later NJO-era burdens and tragedy become active.
